name: Shaolin Kung Fu
notes: Chess and Shaolin Kung Fu are a way of life! With Chess, just as in Kung Fu, controlling the center controls the match. Your opponent will need to go around your defenses; their attack will take more time, and you will gain the initiative with faster counterattacks. Both in Chess and in Kung Fu the road to mastery takes persistence, tenacity, and imagination. It is the literal translation of the Word Kung Fu, which means "Time and Effort" or rather "Mastery of a skill, through time and effort." While I have achieved the level of Elder Master "Lau Shifu" in Shaolin Kung Fu I have yet to attain the level of Master in Chess, but I will continue to enjoy the journey along the way, and hopefully a few of you will join me on this lifelong endeavor. Come try our Shaolin Chess Club out, and if you want you can look up Shaolin Kung Fu at Shao-lin.com to start your Shaolin Kung Fu journey as well. djemir.com If you want to play over the board, live chess games, we also have a Chess Club every Friday from 4PM-5:45PM at The Tallyn's Reach Library in Aurora, Colorado.

name: Plaza Midwood Chess Club
notes: Plaza Midwood Chess Club ♟️ is the place where chess enthusiasts gather to enjoy the game without the fuss of ratings or formal education. Our club is all about connecting with like-minded people, fostering a stronger community, and making new friends. Chess is the excuse! Here, it’s all about easygoing matches mixed with the occasional eureka moment when inspiration strikes! If you're an adult in the Charlotte area looking forward to make some new friends and play some Chess PMCC is your perfect match. Grab your chess gear, bring your personable self, and get ready for a cool, fun, and friendly chess experience! Whether you're a newbie figuring out your first moves or Magnus Carlsen, everyone’s welcome to join in the fun. Feel free to engage in games with or without time controls, whatever suits your style. We’ve got some chess sets ready for action, but you’re totally free to bring your own setup, including a chess clock if timed games are your thing. We'll keep it classy with chess etiquette, no trash talk needed. Find us on: 📷 instagram.com/plazamidwood_chessclub 🎟️ meetup.com/plaza-midwood-chess-club

name: First City Chess - USCF Unrated
notes: This is the official Chess.com page for First City Chess Club (FC3) . We are a local chess club based in Leavenworth, Kansas , currently in the process of building out both our in-person playing location and our over-the-board tournament schedule . This Chess.com club serves as an open, unrated online space for players of all ages and skill levels to connect, play, and participate in online events. It is designed to be welcoming to new, casual, and scholastic players, as well as adult players who simply want to play chess and be part of the local community. Please note that membership in the official USCF-rated First City Chess Club is separate and requires rating verification. This club exists to make participation easy and accessible while the organization continues to grow.

name: First City Chess Club - USCF Rated
notes: This is the Private Chess.com page for the USCF Affiliate "First City Chess Club (A9754602)". Verified membership with USCF is required to join this page. You will need to first be vetted as a member with the US Chess Federation. Please validate using this Google Form https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeOcUdiFZDIbNeRNp2cdRw-4BI4xwQIxJlYN6jwiO8eQ4qrbw/viewform?usp=dialog USCF validation will occur on a monthly basis. If your membership will expire in less than 15 days from the monthly verification, you will be removed from the group until your USCF status is current. All tournaments hosted on this site will be USCF-Online-Rated. All times listed in all event listings and announcements are Central Standard Time (CST) This homepage is the official chess.com page for the First City Chess Club (FC3). We are a local club in Leavenworth, Kansas. We currently meet at KCKCC Annex, 1901 Spruce Street, Room 146, Leavenworth, KS 66048. We meet most Thursdays, from 6:00-8:30 PM, with the exception of Thanksgiving and the two Thursdays before and after Christmas. More Information is available on Https://firstcitychess.com

The purpose of the El Dorado Hills & Folsom Chess Club on Chess.com is to enable our members to play each other and potentially other Clubs online in an arena format. This gives the Club the flexibility to play games online and OTB on a specific day, time and duration. This online format also gives more opportunities for club members to enjoy playing chess with one another.

name: Turkish American Chess Community of Washington
notes: Welcome to the Turkish American Chess Community of Washington! This club brings together chess lovers from the Turkish-American community in the Seattle area. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced player, you’re in the right place to enjoy games, improve your skills, and connect with others. Group Rules: Be respectful and kind to one another. Keep conversations friendly and inclusive. No offensive, inappropriate, or political content. Absolutely no cheating — use of chess engines or outside assistance during games is not allowed and goes against the spirit of the game. Play fair — good sportsmanship is expected. This is a community space — help us keep it positive and fun! We’re excited to have you with us. Let’s enjoy honest, respectful, and fun chess together! Washington Türk Amerikan Satranç Topluluğu’na hoş geldiniz! Bu kulüp, Seattle bölgesindeki Türk-Amerikan topluluğundaki satranç severleri bir araya getiriyor. İster yeni başlayan olun ister deneyimli bir oyuncu, burada oyun oynamak, gelişmek ve yeni insanlarla tanışmak için doğru yerdesiniz. Topluluk Kuralları: Birbirimize saygılı ve nazik olalım. Sohbetler dostça ve kapsayıcı olsun. Küfürlü, uygunsuz ya da siyasi içerik paylaşmayalım. Hileye kesinlikle yer yok — satranç motoru ya da dış yardım kullanmak oyunun ruhuna aykırıdır ve kabul edilmez. Adil oynayalım — sportmenlik bizim için önemlidir. Burası bir topluluk alanı — ortamın pozitif ve keyifli kalmasına hep birlikte dikkat edelim. Aramıza katıldığınız için çok mutluyuz. Haydi dürüst, saygılı ve keyifli maçlarla satrançın tadını birlikte çıkaralım!

name: Memento Rex
notes: Memento Rex is more than a chess club — it's a brotherhood of thinkers, tacticians, and leaders who embrace the beauty of the game and the weight of the crown. Rooted in strategy, discipline, and legacy, our club is where moves matter and minds sharpen. Whether you’re a grandmaster in the making or just learning how to castle, you’re welcome here. We meet weekly to play, learn, and grow — on the board and in life. Tournaments, deep dives into strategy, occasional mic’d-up blitz battles, and off-the-board conversations that hit just as hard. 64 squares. Infinite possibilities. Always remember the King.

name: Bartow Co. GA Chess Club
notes: Welcome to the chess club for Bartow County, Georgia! We’re here to build a local chess community—online and (hopefully) over the board. Join us for friendly games, tournaments, and conversation with fellow chess enthusiasts in the area. Let’s put Bartow on the chess map—one move at a time! PS - if you know the guy who started the other Bartow county club, please tell him we need to merge or something. I made this club because his looked inactive and I wanted to join.

name: Utqiagvik Chess Club
notes: Welcome to the northernmost chess community in the United States! The Utqiagvik Chess Club is a close-knit group of players from all walks of life — students, elders, professionals, and lifelong locals — united by a shared love for the game. Our remote Arctic town may be far from the rest of the world, but the spirit of competition and camaraderie is alive and well around our boards. Whether you’re a seasoned player or just learning how the pieces move, you’ll find a welcoming space here. We gather casually throughout the year for fun matches and friendly rivalry, and each spring we host our Annual Piraaguaqta Chess Tournament , where tradition, strategy, and community pride come together. Join us, make a move, and be part of something special — right here in Utqiagvik.

name: King Sacrifice PBC Chess Club
notes: King Sacrifice is the Chess Club of Purcellville Baptist Church (PBC). We're open to everyone, whether you're a seasoned chess player or don't know "how the horsey thing moves." After opening prayer, players can go straight to the boards (drop-in chess). For anyone interested in the more structured aspects of a chess club, we'll have a short video lesson followed by puzzles/tactics, then open play. Instruction for children and newcomers is available. We meet 1st and 3rd Wednesday of the month from 6:30-8:30 at PBC. NOTE: You do not need to be a Christian or a member of PBC to attend King Sacrifice. This club will have regular devotionals and prayer time. Apart from that, we're here to play/learn chess and have a good time!

name: Kodiak Chess Masters
notes: Welcome to the Kodiak Chess Masters , proudly based on Kodiak Island—home to the longest history of chess playing in Alaska! Our club is a vibrant community that unites players from all walks of life, including local businesses, the Coast Guard, teachers, doctors, fish plant workers, seiner fleet captains, Kodiak High School students, and Seminarians from St. Herman Theological Seminary. Whether you're a seasoned master or a curious beginner, you'll find a welcoming space to grow, compete, and enjoy the timeless game of chess with us. Join us and become part of Kodiak's rich chess tradition. With some serious chess brains at work, there’s always a challenge waiting! We gather every Saturday from 7 to 9PM at Saint Herman Seminary, 414 Mission Road , Alaska’s oldest educational institution, to play, learn, and share our passion for chess in the unique and historic setting of Kodiak Island.
address: 414 Mission Road, Saint Herman Seminary, Kodiak, AK

name: PDX Chess - Adult Learners
notes: PDX Chess is vibrant and engaging gathering designed for adults who share a passion for the game of chess. Hosted at venues Portland Oregon, this meetup provides a welcoming environment where chess enthusiasts of all skill levels come together to learn, play, and connect. If you are BAND NEW or people that want to lean, check out our quarterly teaching days, or come to any meeting and let the host know. We will teach the very basics. Whether you're a beginner eager to grasp the fundamentals or an experienced player looking to refine your strategy, this meetup offers a diverse range of activities tailored to your needs. Engage in friendly matches, participate in chess puzzles and tactics discussions, or join interactive workshops led by seasoned chess instructors. The meetup fosters a sense of community, encouraging participants to share their knowledge, experiences, and insights. It's a fantastic opportunity to make new friends who share your love for the game and to exchange strategies that can elevate your chess skills. The PDX Chess Adult Learners club is a supportive and intellectually stimulating space where adults can expand their understanding of the game while enjoying the camaraderie of fellow players. So whether you're seeking to enhance your chess prowess or simply looking for an enjoyable way to spend your evenings and meet new people, this meetup welcomes you with open arms.
address: Portland Chess Center, 2025 Lloyd Center, Portland, OR 97232

name: Delmar Public Library Youth Chess Club
notes: My name is Brian Banks, a.k.a. coachbrian. I am the founder and administrator of the Delmar Library Online Chess Club and a USCF-certified chess instructor. Our club promotes and teaches chess to kids and adults aged six and above in Delmar and the surrounding area. Our goal is to help people learn and improve their chess skills and to have fun. Chess is more than just a game; it's a powerful educational tool that enhances critical thinking, problem-solving skills, and strategic planning. Studies have shown that students engaged in chess develop improved concentration, memory, and creativity. These cognitive skills acquired through chess transcend the game and prove invaluable in academic pursuits and real-life situations.

name: The Knight's Order
notes: The Cottondale Hornets Chess Club in Cottondale, Florida, is a vibrant community of chess enthusiasts dedicated to promoting the game of chess and fostering strategic thinking among its members. From beginners to experienced players, the club welcomes individuals of all ages and skill levels to participate in friendly competition, improve their chess skills, and build lasting friendships. Meetings are held regularly at a local venue, providing a supportive environment where members can learn from each other, share strategies, and engage in challenging matches. With experienced players offering guidance and coaching, beginners have the opportunity to develop their understanding of the game, while seasoned players can refine their skills and strategies through practice and competition. The Cottondale Hornets Chess Club hosts a variety of events, including casual game nights, organized tournaments, and instructional workshops, catering to the diverse interests and preferences of its members. Whether you're looking to sharpen your tactics, meet fellow chess enthusiasts, or simply enjoy the thrill of the game, the club offers something for everyone. Joining the Cottondale Hornets Chess Club is not just about playing chess; it's about becoming part of a supportive community united by a passion for the game and a commitment to intellectual growth and camaraderie.

name: Profe's Chess club
notes: Profe's Chess Club is an esteemed institution dedicated to the promotion and advancement of chess in both its competitive and recreational aspects. Nestled in the heart of the community, it serves as a haven for chess enthusiasts of all ages and skill levels, from beginners to seasoned masters. Founded by a passionate chess aficionado known simply as "Profe," the club embodies his vision of fostering a vibrant chess culture and nurturing the talents of aspiring players. Location: Situated in a cozy yet spacious venue, Profe's Chess Club exudes an atmosphere of intellectual stimulation and camaraderie. The club's location is carefully chosen to be accessible to its members, with ample parking and convenient public transport links. Facilities: The club boasts state-of-the-art facilities tailored to cater to the needs of its diverse membership. Equipped with tournament-grade chess sets, digital chess clocks, and comfortable seating arrangements, the playing area provides an ideal environment for serious games and friendly matches alike. Additionally, the club features a well-stocked chess library, offering a treasure trove of instructional books, classic game collections, and chess periodicals for members to peruse at their leisure. Programs and Activities: Profe's Chess Club offers a comprehensive array of programs and activities designed to engage and inspire chess enthusiasts of all ages and skill levels. Weekly group lessons led by experienced instructors provide invaluable insights into various aspects of the game, covering everything from opening theory and middle game strategy to endgame techniques and tactical motifs. For those seeking personalized instruction, private coaching sessions are available upon request, allowing students to receive individualized guidance tailored to their specific needs and goals. In addition to its instructional offerings, the club hosts a vibrant calendar of events and tournaments throughout the year. From casual blitz and rapid tournaments to more prestigious classical events, there's something for everyone to enjoy. Special themed events and simultaneous exhibitions featuring guest grandmasters add an extra layer of excitement and intrigue, drawing chess enthusiasts from far and wide to participate and spectate. Community Engagement: Beyond its role as a hub for competitive play and skill development, Profe's Chess Club is deeply committed to fostering a sense of community and camaraderie among its members. Regular social events such as pizza nights, movie screenings, and chess-themed parties provide opportunities for players to relax, unwind, and forge lasting friendships with like-minded individuals who share their passion for the game. Moreover, the club actively collaborates with local schools, community centers, and charitable organizations to promote chess education and outreach initiatives, instilling a love of the game in future generations and enriching the broader community. Overall, Profe's Chess Club stands as a beacon of excellence and innovation in the world of chess, embodying the timeless values of sportsmanship, intellectual pursuit, and lifelong learning. Whether you're a novice eager to learn the ropes or a seasoned competitor seeking to hone your skills, there's no better place to experience the joy and beauty of chess than at Profe's Chess Club.

name: Van TX Chess Club
notes: We start at 6pm on Friday, meeting at: Wendy's 133 County Road 314 Unit 100 Van TX Boards (and possibly clocks) are on loan from Lindale Chess Club and possibly Mineola Chess Club - The Tuesday Knights until we get our own equipment. The club is run by President James Wolfe but was launched Nikolaj Kaluderovic and his Van High School Chess Club but is open to all students and members of the community at large. Read the event post for more details. Come out to have some good games and snack or eat if desired. Typical club play is casual; typically you are committed to making a move once you let go. We want friendly games however so you if you and your opponent agree BEFORE a game starts you can choose to play "touch move", to agree to some takebacks, to play a timed game with a clock, etc. Chess variations such as "duck chess", "atomic chess", "bug house", etc. are all allowed -- it's a public venue and all legal behavior is permissible! -- but may not be encouraged. We strive to improve everyone's regular chess skills, which are often complicated enough! Our Facebook page: facebook.com/groups/vantxchessclub/

Sin City Chess Club members(on Chess.com) who also Join the Discord(see link above) will be invited to monthly cash prize tournaments with no entry fee! Along with other perks that go along with being a part of the Sin City Chess Discord Community like: access to voice channels where you can play your favorite chess mode, blitz/bullet/rapid, etc., virtually in one of our 'Playrooms' with your opponent, just as though you were sitting across the table from them! receive invites to other live events in and outside of the Las Vegas community You are joining at the ground level as we are a small new community so you will be one of the original members. name: Foxcliff Chess Club
notes: July 2022 was the start of our inaugural year, and the Club is maturing in many positive ways. People have enjoyed the unique atmosphere of playing chess at the spacious and historic 1920s style Mansion of the Barbasol Shaving Cream founder which has a beautiful view looking over Foxcliff Lake. Inside, the architecture is something to behold or play outside with a stunning view of the estate. In addition, we provide the No. 2 Best Tasting Water in the United States and snacks. In the winter, we provide boards near a fireplace to enhance the experience. If you’ve not seen the mansion, ask one of the Club’s officers for a tour, which will include seeing a knight and the viewing of a vicious murder site, motivated by jealously. Free parking is immediately in front of the Mansion.

name: New Iberia Chess Club
notes: New Iberia Chess Club was founded in 2023 by father and son, Darren and Aren Horton. With a passion for the game and wanting to share that passion with the community; New Iberia Chess Club was created. The mission of our club is a very simple one. Share the game and grow together as a family and community. We welcome all skill levels and ages. Especially our youth. Chess is a essentially a game of life. We need to harness the ability to evaluate our position, calculate attacks and defenses. Become resourceful whenever we fall behind, and in my opinion most important thinking outside of the box. Tactical. Completely fooling your opponent with a beautiful sacrifice our winning ideas that your opponent never sees coming! If this statement seems like the kind of thing that excites you then please join our club and learn more. Weekly meetups here locally and monthly USCF(United States chess federation) rated events. We looking forward to seeing you and your family soon!

name: Traveling Chess Club
notes: Welcome to Traveling Chess Club! A community-based chess club that travels to different locations to play. Our mission is to inspire more people to play chess over the board, whether they've played for years or have never played before. Weekly OTB (Over the board) games, socials and special events! Explore: Our chess club is all about exploring new places, new opponents, and new strategies. We believe that by venturing out and trying new things, we’ll continue to grow and improve as people and players. Play: Chess is a game of strategy and skill, and we believe in playing to win. Playing to your strengths can not only improve your skills in game but also in life. Live: For us, chess is more than just a game - it's a lifestyle. By embracing the lessons and values of the game, we strive to live our lives with integrity, focus, and determination. GenSpace is easily accessible by bus or the Metro D Line (formerly the Purple Line). https://audreyirmaspavilion.org/ The GenSpace Chess Club is where older adults can safely meet to learn and play chess inside our beautiful GenSpace facility. Come join us to connect with fellow chess enthusiasts and make new friends! GenSpace also offers many other daily programs, activities and events for all its members. You get chess and so much more! There’s no place in L.A. quite like it! https://www.annenberggenspace.org/ Chess Club Newcomers: Please call to make an appointment on Wednesdays between 12:30 pm - 2:30 pm to take a tour of GenSpace and to meet our friendly club members. Have questions about our chess club? Feel free to message me anytime. You may also click “Join” to become an online member for free and receive the latest chess club announcements, news and updates. For GenSpace questions, please call GenSpace to speak with our wonderful staff. GenSpace contact info can be found by clicking the link above. Thank you for your interest. I hope to see you soon! name: Finn Chess Tournaments
notes: Competitive Chess Tournaments in the Capital Region. We meet at the Shelly Studio of Bridge and Games, 6 British American Blvd, Suite 103, Latham, NY 12110. Named after the former New York State Champion and Tournament Organizer, Julius Finn, ( https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Julius_Finn), Finn Chess Tournaments will continue the great efforts of the many predecessors who have promoted and provided chess to players of all abilities in New York State. The years-long tradition of weekend chess begun by the late Norma Shelly will now live on in her namesake studio. _____________________________________ Main issues with amateur chess: 1) Amateur tournaments have become too entwined with money, which only promotes engine-cheating. 2) Non-tournament chess is almost exclusively played on-line. Addressing these issues, Finn Chess Tournaments aims to provide an improved opportunity to play chess in the Northeast. Our goal is to provide low-cost, in-person, rated chess tournaments for all players of all abilities.

name: Rockville Chess Meetup
notes: This chess club is for chess players interested in playing chess in the Rockville, Maryland, USA area, or for people who might be interested in traveling there at some point but who are interested in online interactions as well. Rockville isn't St. Louis , but chess is alive and well in the city. We host the Chesapeake Open . Nothing to sneeze at. Our club offers rated over the board games, un-rated over the board games, and in person chess lessons. For unrated games we flagrantly violate the no-talking rule of tournament chess. After a bad move has been played, by all means let the other player know about it so they can improve from their mistakes on the spot instead of when they barely remember what happened. If you realize you made a bad move, feel free to ask for a takeback. If you are much better than your opponent, offer to remove a few pieces before the game starts. All ages and abilities are welcome. As a social club, please BYOB. (Board, not beer!) Most club events will be dual posted, but we encourage members to be members here, https://www.meetup.com/Rockville-Chess-Meetup/ , and and https://www.rockvillechess.org/ for more information or tournament dates. The privacy settings of this club are set to secret. This means that only members are able to view posts or know who the other members are. Let's have fun and get better!

There will be about 10 Saturday tournaments in a year in either Jefferson City or Fulton, MO. name: 29 Palms Chess Club
notes: About Twentynine Palms Chess Club Welcome to the Twentynine Palms Chess Club, a community for chess enthusiasts in and around Twentynine Palms! We proudly serve both local residents and military personnel stationed at the Marine Corps Air Ground Combat Center. Whether you live in the area or have a deep appreciation for Twentynine Palms, we invite you to join our growing community of chess players. Our goal is to foster a friendly and competitive chess environment where members can improve their skills and enjoy the game. Weather permitting, we hope to host weekly or monthly in-person meetings at local parks like Knotts Sky Park or Luckie Park, depending on interest and participation. No matter your skill level, you’re welcome here. Come play, learn, and connect with fellow chess lovers as we build a vibrant chess scene in Twentynine Palms!

Like the old English Pub, except with hot chocolate/coffee instead of beer, and chess instead of darts. Players of all strengths participate. The more experienced players tend to take a mentoring/teaching role to the less experienced players. It is a place where taking a move back is not frowned upon, thus the name of the club being 'The Mulligan Chess Club' (mulligan being a golf term for hitting the ball again, without a penalty, if you did not like your shot). We meet every Saturday from 2 PM to 7 PM at: Avery Muirfield Panera 6665 Perimeter Loop Rd Dublin, OH 43017 Join this free chess club at https://www.meetup.com/mulligan-columbus-chess-club/

Another Kauai site on chess.com is a club called Team Kauai, for those who wish to participate in matches with other islands, states, colleges, and countries. Please spread the word and invite other players from Kauai. The active sites on Chess.com for people who wish to play team matches for Hawaii are Hawaii Chess Federation and Team Hawaii . The active site for K-12 school-aged students is HCF Scholastic . Please invite other players from Kauai.

name: Right Track Mindz
notes: During this time, we are looking for more to engage our children that is positive… and not Fortnite. Chess Club is an activity that has many benefits and is also fun! Academic Improvement: Chess helps to improve a child’s academic performance. Academically, we aren’t just talking about Math improvement, as some people mistakenly assume. Studies often show improvement in all academic areas - Math, English, Reading, Science, and so on. The reason for this is that chess doesn’t directly teach us math - chess improves our Executive Functions . Chess gives children better control of their own brains - more organized thoughts, better planning, better visualization, and better focus. These are skills that can be used on anything. The result is more efficient and effective learning in general.

name: Hub City Chess Club KY
notes: We are a USChess affiliated club. You must be a current member of USChess to belong to our online club and to participate in our USChess rated live tournaments. Membership in our club and eligibility to play in our USChess rated tournaments requires that you follow the instructions below to verify your USChess membership and apply to join the USChess - Members Only club. In order to compete in any US Chess Online rated event, players are required to be an active US Chess member and have filled out the form below. For weekly games, the official US Chess - Members Only club runs regular online rated events. Please allow 48 to 72 hours for US Chess to verify your US Chess ID and PIN. By registering your Chess.com username with your US Chess ID, US Chess is able to ensure you are protected from rating fraud. For a faster processing time, we recommend requesting to join the club after filling out the application. You only need to fill out the form once to gain eligibility, but if you change your username you may need to resubmit the form to renew your authentication. If you do not know your PIN, check the back of your Chess Life magazine. If you do not have a Chess Life magazine subscription, please visit the US Chess PIN Request Center and fill out the required information. Upon completion, US Chess will send your PIN to the email they have on file for you. Looking for a Game? Connect with other US Chess Members and play online rated games with players across the United States. Join

name: AZ Chess Central
notes: At Arizona Chess Central our mission is to deliver the best chess opportunities for all scholastic chess players by providing high quality low cost chess tournaments, chess camps, chess instruction and chess activities. Arizona Chess Central holds monthly tournaments for players of all ages (Kindergarten through Adult) and offers free game review at each and every tournament. Game review is performed by our top level coaching staff and is available to all players, young and old - beginner to expert. Adult participation is welcomed and encouraged. We have sections available at every tournament to provide an opportunity for adult play as well as to strengthen the playing abilities of some of the stronger scholastic players. Arizona Chess Central offers chess lessons and chess education classes for kids of all ages. Our "Friday Knight Frenzy", with Blitz and Bughouse, draws both adult and scholastic chess enthusiasts and is an excellent opportunity to sharpen your chess skills. We are not a club and there are no members. Everyone is Welcome! All chess enthusiasts, from beginner to expert, are eligible to participate in our chess events and activities and enjoy the services we provide.

name: Social Chess in the Mission
notes: Come for the chess; stay for the fun people and conversations, good food, beer and wine. Ideal players are adults who know at least the basic rules, but don't have to take the game too seriously. You should have a very relaxed attitude about winning/losing. Just for fun, fun, fun, and the chance to learn and/or stay in practice. If you have a chess set (board and pieces) and/or a chess clock, it'd be helpful if you bring it. But if you don't, that's fine too. (After all, only half the players need to bring a set!) We meet at the Crepe House (1132 Valencia) in the Mission http://goo.gl/yItDVq >, whose management is very welcoming of our group and very generously gives us a large table for our games. Please recognize the Crepe House's generosity by some win-win giving back: consider buying dinner there or, if you're arriving later, maybe dessert (e.g., nutella crepe!); or at least wine/beer/coffee, etc. (Here's their menu: http://goo.gl/u13rY1 .) Please help make sure we'll continue to be welcomed in the future! (It goes without saying that you shouldn't import alien food or drink!)

name: The Delmarva Chess Club
notes: Welcome to The Delmarva Chess Club! We usually have weekly Online Club Matches on Tuesday evenings. Pre-registration starts at 6 pm, and matches begin at 7 pm. Our goal is to complete the tournament by 9 pm. However, depending on the number of participants, it may occasionally be as late as 10 pm. Important note: Minors must have permission from their parent or guardian to join the club and play in tournaments. Also, members may only have one active account in this club. Furthermore, we have our in-person Club meetings on Wednesday evenings at Wor Wic Community College (WWCC) in the new Patricia and Alan Guerrieri Technology Center (GTC), Second floor, Room 204, from 5:30 to 8:30 pm. We provide chess sets and clocks, so you won't need anything. Bring a friend! Here is the address to the WWCC: Wor-Wic Community College, 32000 Campus Dr, Salisbury, MD 21804 If you are interested in Chess and want to learn to play or improve your game and live on the Eastern Shore of Delmarva or the surrounding area, I invite you to join the club. There is absolutely no cost or obligation! If you need help signing up, navigating the website, or have questions, please don't hesitate to contact me. name: Funky Owls Chess
notes: The Funky Owls is a real life club! Our online club is FREE. Funky Owls Online Club Registration Form Welcome! We would love to have you! Tournaments may require a separate registration & fees. Our LIVE Community Chess Events are free! (Follow us on Facebook for the schedule.) A ”fun” tournament is not USCF rated. Meaning it will not be an official rating, only chess website ratings and/or local club ratings. USCF Rated tournaments or sections (within a tournament) require an active USCF membership and ID. Those tournaments or sections will upload to the USCF database. Help keep this community a safe & sociable online gathering! Report anyone that makes you feel uncomfortable. Inappropriate behavior will not be tolerated! This is a family friendly community. Your request to join our online club will be reviewed prior to acceptance. Fill out a registration form per person. You will not be admitted without having established a connection with me or a real life club member. Thank you! Funky Owls Online Club Registration Form (copy of above link) *Privacy Policy: We will not share your information with ANYBODY in any way! name: Chess Players Anonymous (Boston Meetup)
notes: http://www.meetup.com/Chess-Players-Anonymous-CPA/ Hello people, welcome to our new official chess.com group. This group is primarily for the members of our Boston Meetup group. Our main priority is to play in person. This online thing is just a backup to prevent withdrawal symptoms. CPA is about reenergizing our passion for chess and having lots of fun in the process. CPA is meant to be an aggregation of chess players throughout the Boston area who probably do many other things but still maintain a passion for chess. On a bi-weekly basis, members will come together to challenge each other to friendly chess matches and conversation. Tournaments will be organized as membership allows.

name: Let's Castle
notes: Enter the captivating realm of chess with Lets Castle, a vibrant and welcoming club where fun, friendship, and healthy competition thrive! Participate in exciting daily online tournaments on (chess.com), weekly blitz and rapid events, and live over-the-board chess tournaments- all driven by a passion for the game and the thrill of victory. Players of all levels, from seasoned masters to curious newcomers, are invited to join our community. Additionally, beginner players can enjoy lessons on the basics of chess on scheduled Saturdays 2:30pm till 5pm pm (at the Hillcrest Library or Cunningham Park near the tennis courts) in the New York City area. Become a part of Lets Castle and discover the delight of chess - a game that's sure to inspire your mind and …MAKE YOU FEEL GOOD!

name: Selmer Chess Club

Ultimately,we see chess as a tool for self-improvement,development of our community and as a bridge to other communities. We, the members of the (SCC) are dedicated to promote good moral conduct,mutual respect,and good sportsman conduct. It is also our goal to promote a healthly network of local area clubs as well as with clubs in other cities. We intend on preparing for and participating in tournament competition and encourage membership in the United States Chess Federation. name: Phoenix Chess and Coffee Meetup
notes: Note: Please visit our site (Phoenix Chess and Coffee) on meetup.com to join the group and RSVP When: Each Saturday, from 3PM until around 7PM (although sometimes it goes on for longer)Play chess and drink coffee with our group at the Coffee Plantation off of Shea Blvd, in Scottsdale! The Coffee Plantation is located near the movie theater behind the Butterfields pancake restaurant, so it's pretty easy to find. There's a connected Gelato shop next to it, too, for those who are feeling adventuresome. Chess meetups usually last a couple of hours. If you have a good chess board, please bring it! A lot of people like to bring their chess clocks, too. If you don't have a chess board, you're still welcome to come (technically, only half of the people have to bring boards for everyone to be able to play). Please note: This is a fixed, regular weekly meetup, so even though only a couple of people might RSVP, usually anywhere from 10-20 people show up every week, so keep that in mind. If at all possible, please RSVP, as this helps to show members how popular the meetup actually is! Thanks! We don't have a membership fee, and our meetup group really only has one rule that we ask: Please don't take the game too seriously. Winning and losing doesn't matter here. We're not playing rated games, and this isn't a tournament. Just have fun with it, and make some friends! If you do this, I guarantee you will not only have a blast, but will most likely improve your chess game over time without even realizing it! One secondary note I should make (although it isn't really a rule), is that as long as we keep buying drinks/snacks/etc and showing our friendliness and patronage towards the Coffee Plantation, they will continue to welcome us, so please keep that in mind. If we bring drinks/food with us from other places or only ever order things like water, it's only a matter of time before our group would end up without a home... :-\7366 East Shea BoulevardScottsdale, AZ, 85260www.meetup.com/coffeechess/Contact Email:

name: Metro Phoenix Chess Club - Arizona Chess
notes: The purpose of the Metro Phoenix Chess Club is to provide a forum in which chess players of all ages and skill levels can exercise their talents and develop into chess players of excellent caliber and character, and to promote the study and knowledge of chess; not only for fun and enjoyment, but also as an art form, and as a means for the improvement of society. In addition, we aim to provide the metro Phoenix area with "quality" venues for weekly chess playing. Our events will consist of fee based USCF tournaments and free casual / informal chess. We will host occasional annual 2-day and 3-day weekend open tournaments, and provide small group and private chess lessons. We strive for chess excellence here at the Metro Phoenix Chess Club ( MPCC ), a not-for-profit organization where quality and character counts. name: Boyston Chess Club
notes: The Boylston Chess Club of the Boylston Chess Foundation is the largest chess club in Boston, and the third oldest chess organization in the United States. Starting in the 1850s the Boston Young Men's Christian Union (YMCU) maintained a small room for chess; John F. Barry, Harry Nelson Pillsbury, C.F. Burille, Franklin K. Young and Walcott frequented it in the 1890s. The club was formally organized on August 27, 1919 at the YMCU. There were 22 charter members, and Augustus Seaver was elected as the first Club President. In its history, many leading chess figures have been Club President, including Master Emeritus Harry Lyman. In 1945, the club received charter number 51 from the newly formed United States Chess Federation. In 1989, facing sharply increased rent and deteriorating facilities, the Boylston Chess Club moved to the 8th floor of the YWCA at 140 Clarendon St. in Boston. The club was incorporated as a Massachusetts non-profit corporation in 1995. In December 2003, the YWCA's building renovation plans forced the club to move to a new home in Davis Square, Somerville. The Boylston Chess Foundation -- the 501(c)(3) organization -- was founded in 2005. The Boylston Chess Club and Foundation is not just a historic club from the 19th century, but also a flourishing chess center in 21st century Boston and one of the most active clubs in the nation. Since 1992, the Boylston has organized more than 1252 rated chess tournaments. In 2008, 390 different players played in 133 tournaments. At the end of 2008 we had 164members, and throughout 2008 we had 238 different people as members. Members and players at the Club in 2008 included 33 chess masters (including GMs, IMs, and SMs - 20 of whom are members) and 23 experts.240 Elm St., Suite B9Somerville, MA, 02144(617) 629-3933www.boylstonchessclub.org/Contact Email: boylstonchess@gmail.com
address: 240 Elm St, Suite B9, Somerville, MA 02144

name: Winchester Chess Club2
notes: The Winchester Chess Club was formed in the Spring of 1992. Connie Stolow, an educational consultant to gifted students, had several parents who wanted to know if there was a chess club in the area where their children could learn chess. Connie contacted Harold Dondis, the chess correspondent to the Boston Globe who put her in touch with Dr. Michael Charney. Dr. Charney started The Games Project/Chess Makes Kids Smart. Connie and Dr. Charney came up with some ideas on how to start up a chess club in Winchester. Connie approached the Winchester Library about using one of the basement rooms and posted chess board notices around town inviting people to an organizational meeting. Although Connie did not play chess herself, the notices attracted several experienced chess players who began holding chess games and lessons. The Games Project/Chess Makes Kids Smart established many neighborhood chess programs and distributed chess sets. It also started a program to design and construct giant chess sets. With a grant from Enka, the Winchester Chess Club participated in building a giant chess set that is still available for play on Town Day in the Winchester Chess Club area. The club met on Saturday mornings at the library until January 1993. But, due to sports, many students could not play chess on Saturdays. The library could not provide space late enough in the evenings. So Connie found out that the Town Hall was available to the Chess Club at no charge on Friday evenings. Vernon Shoup was the first president. Tom Richardson (currently Tournament Director) was vice president and John Shawcross (currently Treasurer and Secretary) were members from the first year. Neil Akiyama was also active in membership and and publicity during the early day of the club. David Plantamura (currently President) joined a few years later. Steve Frymer of Lexington who was MACA President also came by the club from time to time to encourage it to develop and include more tournaments. In the early days, Tom Richardson and sometimes Steve Frymer would do a simultaneous exhibition playing up to 20 players. On January 8, 1993 the club moved to the Winchester Town Hall. Initially using the Winchester Room and expanding to a second basement level room as membership grew. Since the Town Hall was free, the club was a low cost operation. No membership dues were charged although small entry fees were charged for tournaments in order to provide prizes and to purchase trophies. A coffee can was available for donations and gradually chess sets and boards were purchased. Some parents started bringing soda and selling it and this enabled the club to buy chess clocks. Later on the ENKA society provided a grant to buy digital clocks, which are still in use in 2008. In the fall of 2004, after more than 11 years, the Town decided that the club could no longer play free of charge at the Town Hall. It would require the Chess Club to pay for the janitor who would be on overtime. The club could not afford that, so we looked at several alternatives, finally settling on the Parish of the Epiphany at 70 Church Street Winchester. We played there for the first time on October 8, 2004. The church charged the club to recover the costs of heating the church so it became necessary to charge a $25 fee per player each half season. The members loved the new location with its gothic charm. The parents also enjoyed having an area with couches and soft chairs, where they could relax and socialize, while their children played chess. In late 2007, Jim Herbert heard that the Griffin Museum at 67 Shore Road Winchester would host the Winchester Chess Club at no cost. The club moved on February 1, 2008. This is a beautiful well lit location with the added benefit of providing chess club members an opportunity to view the various exhibits at the Museum. Tournaments and Events: The club holds tournaments at various times throughout the season. These tournaments offer time controls from five minutes for the one week speed tournament to seventy-five minutes for the five week annual club tournament. There is also a bughouse tournament where teams of two players battle it out. In bughouse, teams are chosen by pairing the best players with junior players which means any team has a chance of winning the event. We also hold a Herbert Handicap where based on club ratings the better players remove some of their pieces at the start of the game to equalize the games. The event is named after Jim Herbert who donated an antique silver cocktail shaker which had been a trophy at the Boston Chess Club in 1932 (the Boston Chess Club played Bridge!). Above history compiled in early 2008 by David Plantamura (President), and reviewed by John Shawcross (Treasurer and Webmaster), Tom Richardson (Tournament Director) and Connie Stolow (Founder of the Chess Club). Details were updated April 2008 after Tom Richardson found a copy of the first Winchester Chess Club Newsletter (Volume 1, Number 1 dated January 1993 announcing the schedule and the move to the Town Hall). ( As time permits we may expand this history. if anyone wants to suggest some detail or correct errors please let me know. John Shawcross) 67 Shore Rd.Winchester, MA, 01890781 721-1520winchesterchessclub.club.officelive.com/default.aspxContact Email:

name: Atlanta Chess Mess

Atlanta Chess Mess provides a fun and relaxed place to play chess, eat some food, and drink a beer or some coffee while playing the game we love. We have people of all skill levels. 700-2000. Boys, girls, old, young, black and white, green and gray. We are always looking to grow the group. Regardless of whether you are a master or new to the game, you will likely find someone of your skill level to play competitive games with, and if not, you can teach or be taught. Join our facebook page which is listed on the google site for all updates and information about upcoming meetings and events. name: Right Move Public Chess Club Colorado Springs
notes: Right Move Public Chess Club of Colorado Springs, CO is a weeknight chess club meeting every Thursday evening beginning at 6:00pm: The Right Move Public Chess Club of Colorado Springs is dedicated to the promotion and enjoyment of chess. Our youth and adult members enjoy both tournament style and social chess games within a quiet and pleasant environment.Visitors & new members are always welcome! There are no compulsory membership fees or dues of any kind. Supporters of the club make voluntary donations to help offset club expenses. Come out and join us in one of our club competitions or just come for an informal, 'friendly' game of chess. The club offers group lessons off-site on Monday evenings. Most club activities are free. Occasionally we will have tournaments or competitions which will serve as fundraisers for the club. Club doors open on Thursdays at 6:00pm for social & casual chess games. Tournament style play starts at 6:30pm unless otherwise noted. Informal 'friendly' games are available anytime during club hours until we close at 9:30pm. We hope you will sincerely find the club to be a congenial place for you to come and play the Royal Game of chess. At our weekly meetings you will find many chess players of all abilities and interests, from novice to intermediate to advanced. Whether you prefer just a casual game or two or are here for serious tournament play, we can accommodate you. The Right Move Public Chess Club of Colorado Springs has one weekly meeting for pick-up games and tournament style play and a separate weekly meeting off-site for group chess lessons. Thursday Club Meeting: 6:00 to 9:30pm Monday Group Lessons: 7:00 to 10:00pm (off-site) Please contact us if you are interested in joining the club, or just dropping by every once in a while, so that we may plan accordingly. Becoming a member is simply a matter of saying "I want to play chess at Right Move Public Chess Club". There are no compulsory membership fees or dues of any kind. Just come as you are and be ready to play chess. Some chess sets & clocks will be provided - Bring your own just in case! name: Mulligan Chess Club
notes: Located in downtown Worthington, this club has a very relaxed flavor to it. A place where anyone is welcome to come, sip on a drink, relax, and not feel intimidated. It is a place where taking a move back is not frowned upon, thus the name of the club being 'The Mulligan Chess Club' (mulligan being a golf term for hitting the ball again, without a penalty, if you did not like your shot). name: Kenilworth Chess Club
notes: Established in 1972, the Kenilworth Chess Club is a fun and active club with both beginner and master chess players. We meet every Thursday night beginning at 8:00 p.m. in the Recreation Building at 575 Kenilworth Boulevard in Kenilworth, New Jersey. Enter through the rear door. The building is easy to find at the corner of 24th Street and less than a mile west of Garden State Parkway Exit 138 (see complete directions and map on our website). It is to the left of the Kenilworth Municipal Building, which houses the Kenilworth Police. Visitors and new members are always welcome and chess lessons are available. Membership is $15 per year. Membership is not required to play, but some activities (such as our annual Club Championship tournament) are open only to members. Chess sets, boards, and clocks are provided. Most meetings feature casual play and discussion. We also often host lectures, rated and unrated tournaments, team matches, and other chess events. See our club calendar for details.575 Kenilworth BoulevardKenilworth, NJ, 07033www.kenilworthchessclub.org/Contact Email:

name: Queen City Chess Association
notes: The Queen City Chess Association (USCF Affiliate A6029063) exists to promote the game of chess in the Charlotte, NC area. Our goals are: 1) To provide Charlotte area chess players of all ages and skill levels the chance to compete and develop friendships with other community players. 2) To promote the game within the Charlotte area; develop methods to bring the game to new people, recruit them and retain them as a part of our community. 3) To develop and administer an annual tournament that is the Championship of the Charlotte area. 4) To chronicle our events and, to the best of our ability, retrieve the history of chess in the Charlotte area. 5) To act as a "bridge" organization for junior players to step into adult chess. 6) To develop master and Internationally titled players from within our ranks. If noone is open, lay out your own board and someone will likely come around for a game. Blitz is popular, but there are leisurely players too. Other great places to play for Portland Chess:Pioneer Courthouse Square NW corner of Morrision & Broadwayoutside Starbucks. Lunch till evenings during nice weather. The new Director Park, downtown, has a BIG Chess set. The staff brings the pieces out daily at 2pm. The Portland Chess Club (photos)8205 SW 24th Ave. 503/246-2978 The Portland Chess Club now meets Tue, Thur at 7:30 PM and Saturdays at 2:00 PM. name: Mechanics Institute Chess Club wannabe
notes: The Mechanics Institute Chess Club is the oldest Chess club in America, and one of the oldest in the world. (It was founded in 1854.) It is located in downtown San Francisco, half a block from the Montgomery Street BART Station. The club has hosted many World Chess Champions, including Emanuel Lasker, Jose Raul Capablanca, Alexander Alekhine, Max Euwe, Bobby Fischer, Vasily Smyslov, Tigran Petrosian, Boris Spassky, and Anatoli Karpov. When asked in 1929 where he found the strongest Chessplayers in America, then World Champion Alexander Alekhine replied, "In San Francisco, in a place called the Mechanics' Institute." More about the remarkable history of this club may be learned here: http://www.chessclub.org/History.html and here: http://www.chessclub.org/hist.html The Club is open for casual play from 11am to 9 pm on weekdays and from 11am to 5pm on weekends. Tournament days excepted. Please see the main website ( www.chessclub.org ) for more details.Grandmaster Alex Yermolinsky gives free classes on Tuesday and Wednesday evenings. Other classes are also offered (including special classes for women and for children). More information: http://www.chessclub.org/Calendar.html The Chess Club is a part of the Mechanics Institute Library ( www.milibrary.org ), a private library founded in 1854 with books on a very broad range of subjects. Not surprisingly, the Library has an extensive collection of books on Chess, many of historical interest.Click here for a map the the Mechanics Institute Chess Club. (You may also find the Newsletter and the Tournament Archive interesting. The October 2006 visit of former World Champion Boris Spassky is covered here http://www.chessclub.org/Spassky.html along with a news video about that visit.) Please consider visiting! 57 Post Street4th FloorSan Francisco, CA, 94104(415) 421-2258www.chessclub.orgContact Email: chessroom@milibrary.org
